q: What happens to the rate of photosynthesis as light intensity increases beyond the light saturation point?
options: The rate continues to increase linearly without limit
The rate drops sharply because excess light destroys chlorophyll molecules
The rate plateaus because another factor (CO2 concentration or enzyme activity) becomes limiting
The rate reverses and the plant begins releasing CO2 instead of fixing it
explanation: Once light is no longer limiting, another factor (CO2 level, RuBisCO enzyme capacity, or temperature) becomes the new bottleneck; the rate plateaus until that factor is increased.
